title: Satureja spinosa
taxonomy: Satureja spinosa is classified as a member of the Lamiaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as mint or deadnettle family. It belongs to the genus Satureja, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Mediterranean region. The species Satureja spinosa is further classified as Satureja spinosa, a type of perennial herb that is native to South Africa. Within the Lamiaceae family, Satureja spinosa is closely related to other members of the genus Satureja, such as Satureja montana and Satureja thymbra. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and its relationships with other plants in the Lamiaceae family.
